rows.description: Computus Joannis Conybeare Rectoris a 2do Die Novembris 1730 ad eundem Diem revolutum 1731.
rows.notes: Clear page title at top in Latin script. Date and name legible; very little ambiguity in handwriting or placement.
Centered section heading under the title. Handwriting clear though the Latin spelling is archaic; no amounts on this row.
Very clear numeric trio '10 0 0' aligned with pounds/shillings/pence columns; description legible.

Pounds and shillings (26, 12) clear. Pence reads as '9' with a small looped mark immediately after the pence digit; interpreted as the common half‑penny mark (ob → 0.5). The loop is faint so fraction assignment is moderately uncertain.
Numerals '40 0 0' are well formed and aligned to the three currency columns. Name reading 'Minhinsett' (spelling uncertain) is taken from visible letters.
Large clear '145 0 0' in pounds/shillings/pence columns; 'South Newington' and abbreviation 'Recd.' are clearly readable.
Figures '20 0 0' are unambiguous and well aligned. Name reads as 'Cuddington Recd.' from the script; small uncertainty only in exact abbreviation.
Amount '14 8 10' aligned and clear. Minor ambiguity in the hyphenation/spelling of the place name but amount digits unambiguous.
Amounts read '2 0 0' clearly. Name 'Lucas Eaton' is legible; no fractional marks present.

Amount appears as '14 3 3' and is reasonably clear. The description is less certain — reads as 'Tue Quit Rents' (could be 'The Quit Rents'); labeled an entry because it describes a category rather than a subtotal.
Amounts '0 7 6' are clear and within valid ranges. Description reads 'Ralph Marshals Capons' (period/spacing follows original).
Amount '10 9 11' is legible; pence 11 is valid. Name 'Bigg' clearly written.

Numbers '2 19 5' are clear and shillings (19) within valid range. Description reads 'Calicot alias Lane' though first word might be 'Calicot' or 'Calicot' variant; transcription follows visible letters.
Amounts '0 15 0' clearly aligned. Name 'Bannister' is legible.
Clear '1 0 0' values. The place/name reads as 'Leaden-Hall' with clear letters.
Amount '4 12 11' readable and valid. Name 'Sadler' is clear. No obvious fractional mark after the pence.
Amount '1 13 4' visible though the final pence digit is slightly faded. Description 'Hart Hall' interpreted from script; moderate certainty.

Pounds and shillings '4 1' clear. Pence looks like '8' with a small looped mark after it (similar to the half‑penny sign elsewhere on the page) — interpreted as 8½d. The small loop is faint and could be merely pen flourish, so confidence is moderate.

Amount '4 10 4' is reasonably clear. The name reads as 'Clifton Penny' (the second word is somewhat indistinct — could be 'Perry' or 'Penny'); description recorded as it appears with moderate uncertainty.

Pounds and shillings '7 9' clear. Pence reads '11' and there is a small looped mark after the pence digit (interpreted as a half‑penny sign → 11½d). The loop is faint; fraction assignment is therefore tentative.
Amount '6 0 0' clear. The place name is hard to read; rendered here as 'Prittcheven' based on visible letter shapes — moderate uncertainty in spelling.
Amount '26 10 0' is clear and well aligned. The description contains three names/places separated by commas/et — letters readable though some proper‑name spellings are archaic.
Figures '6 13 4' legible and valid. 'Medenham' reading follows the visible script; there is slight ambiguity in the middle consonants but the amount anchors the row.
Final row amount '4 0 0' clear. Description 'Back Gate, Boyse' is legible; punctuation/spacing retained as on the page.
